purpose: Root tracker for Sybeorg prospect mockups, outreach angles, draft status, and next-batch queue.

positioning.coreAngle: Lead with proof, not pitch: Sybeorg already built a custom landing-page mockup for the prospect, focused on converting emergency/service calls on mobile.
riskGuardrails: Do not imply partnership, endorsement, or that the business requested the mockup.
Avoid quoting unverified review counts or fabricated testimonials in outreach.
Use the phrase sample/mockup/concept so the recipient understands it is a preview, not a live replacement site.
positioning.bestCTA: Keep the ask human and specific: offer to clean up the concept with their real photos, services, and wording after Stark approves the send.
positioning.videoCommercialAngle: SYBEORG can package custom landing pages with professional 30–60 sec business commercials, short vertical video ad cuts, and optional ad launch/management.

currentProspects.angle: The listed website returned HTTP 200 but only a 163-byte blank/placeholder response with no visible title, phone, CTA, services, or emergency route during audit.
currentProspects.qualityDirection: Light HVAC dispatch board with heat/cool triage tickets, blank-site alert banner, and mobile sticky call bar; not the standard contractor hero/form/cards structure.
currentProspects.visualQaNotes: Browser QA on local server returned 200 with no horizontal overflow. Vision QA found the HVAC dispatch concept clear and readable, but slightly polished/AI-SaaS in places; outreach should lead with the blank-site alert first and present the mockup as a no-obligation concept to polish with real local assets.

currentProspects.angle: The listed website failed DNS/name resolution during audit. For truck repair, a broken domain is especially costly because fleet and driver searches tend to be urgent and mobile.
currentProspects.qualityDirection: Dark asphalt/amber fleet dispatch board, lane-style intake cards, and truck-route visual language; intentionally not a residential contractor page.
currentProspects.visualQaNotes: Browser QA on local server returned 200 with no horizontal overflow. Vision QA found the amber dispatch/fleet intake direction distinct and readable, while still concept/pitch-like; outreach should lead with the broken-domain alert first and use the mockup as a sample, with manual contact verification before outreach.

currentProspects.angle: Small-town emergency HVAC page that makes Bobby/owner-operator trust the hero, with phone-first mobile CTA.
currentProspects.qualityDirection: V3 rural dispatch / owner-operator page with warm field-route motif, mobile sticky call dock, and HVAC problem selector.

brandKit.notes: SVG/HTML colors extracted with stdlib; raster logo colors require Pillow if no SVG/logo CSS colors are present.

currentProspects.visualQaNotes: Local QA passed: desktop and 390px mobile have no horizontal overflow; mobile call dock visible; route selector interaction updates copy; console errors clean after inline favicon.

currentProspects.angle: Paused for generic mockup outreach: official site is already credible/polished. Revisit only with a specific conversion audit, paid-ads landing page, video campaign, or service-page expansion angle.
currentProspects.qualityDirection: Do not pitch current mockup as a redesign replacement. Mockup outreach should be reserved for no-site, weak-site, placeholder, broken-flow, or clearly worse-current-site prospects.

currentProspects.angle: The public estimate path appears broken, so high-intent visitors may hit a dead end before requesting service.
currentProspects.designDifferentiation: Light blue/cream estimate-path repair concept with route cards; not dark hero/right-form/cards/reviews.
currentProspects.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed after fixing internal CTA wording: credible light estimate-path concept; clear sample framing; phone marked pending confirmation rather than using an unverified number; no fake proof or generic testimonial blocks.

currentProspects.angle: The site may block some visitors or automated/mobile contexts; a call-first repair page could preserve leads if the public path fails.
currentProspects.designDifferentiation: Service-bay/ticket-board composition; avoids stock mechanic hero, fake certifications, fake reviews, and right-side quote form.
currentProspects.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed after changing badge from ASE to AE to avoid certification ambiguity; credible service-bay/ticket-board concept; layout intact; phone CTA visible; no fake reviews/badges/warranties.

nextBatchQueue.targetMarket: Iowa City / Cedar Rapids / Des Moines
nextBatchQueue.reason: High-ticket commercial/video production offer; strongest fits include restaurants, gyms, med spas, salons, auto/detailing, contractors with visual proof, real estate, venues, nonprofits, and clinics.
High-intent searches, easy before/after website gap spotting, strong template already exists.
Emergency-driven, seasonal urgency, simple landing-page economics.
Higher ticket services and clear booking CTA; needs softer premium design than trades.

gmail.note: Himalaya v1.2.0 can access sybeorg, majidclawbot, and radi Gmail accounts. Outbound sending still requires Stark approval per Hermes safety rules.

brandKit.cleanupNote: Removed brandKit entries that resolved to third-party login pages instead of prospect-owned public sites.
